title: alnus glutinosa var. incana
intro: Alnus glutinosa var. incana, also known as grey alder, is a variety of the alder tree that is native to Europe and Asia. The bark and leaves of the grey alder have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of grey alder include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and ease respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Grey alder is often used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
